items.text:
Maple Street Mobile Kitchen — Weekly Specials

Find us beside Carver Hall, Monday through Friday, 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM.

Monday — Smoky Black Bean Bowl: brown rice, roasted corn, and avocado. $7.50
Tuesday — Crispy Chicken Wrap: grilled chicken, slaw, and chipotle sauce. $8.00
Wednesday — Garden Flatbread: tomato, basil, and fresh mozzarella. $6.50
Thursday — Spiced Lentil Soup with warm sourdough. $5.00
Friday — Korean BBQ Tacos (two per order). $8.50

Student Deal: Show your campus ID and any special is just $5.00, all day, every day.

Add a house lemonade or iced tea for $2.00. Bring your own cup and the drink is free.

We accept cards and the campus meal card only — please note we no longer take cash. Orders placed after 1:45 PM may be limited while supplies last. Follow us on the student portal for daily sell-out updates.

questions.stem: According to the menu, how can a student get any weekly special for $5.00?

options.A: By ordering before 11:00 AM on weekdays
options.B: By showing a campus identification card
options.C: By bringing a personal reusable cup
options.D: By paying with the campus meal card

questions.explanation: The menu states that showing your campus ID makes any special just $5.00, all day, every day. Bringing your own cup applies only to the free drink, and the meal card is one accepted payment method, not the source of the discount.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a customer who arrives at 1:50 PM?

options.A: They will receive a free lemonade with any order
options.B: They might not find every special available
options.C: They must pay an extra fee for late service
options.D: They are required to order from a printed list

questions.explanation: The menu warns that orders placed after 1:45 PM may be limited while supplies last, implying some items could be sold out by 1:50 PM. The other options introduce fees or rules the menu never mentions.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this menu?

options.A: To explain why the truck has stopped taking cash
options.B: To compare prices with other campus restaurants
options.C: To present the truck's weekly specials and policies
options.D: To announce a permanent change in operating hours

questions.explanation: The text lists each day's special, prices, the student deal, and payment rules, so its purpose is to present the weekly specials and policies. The cash policy and supply limits are single details, not the overall point, and no comparison or hours change is announced.

items.text:
Spring Campus Bicycle Rental Program

The Office of Campus Transportation is pleased to announce that the bicycle rental program will reopen for the spring term on March 18. Bicycles may be reserved at the Anderson Hall service desk, which is open Monday through Saturday, 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

Program details are listed below:

- A refundable deposit of $20.00 is required when a bicycle is collected.
- The standard semester pass costs $45.00 and includes a safety helmet.
- Bicycles must be returned by 9:00 PM each day to avoid a $10.00 late fee.
- Helmets are to be worn at all times while riding on campus paths.

Maintenance checks are performed every Friday, so reservations are not accepted on that day. Riders who report a mechanical problem promptly will not be charged for repairs.

For questions, contact the Office of Campus Transportation at transport@campus.edu or visit Room 112 in Anderson Hall.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what does the $45.00 semester pass include?

options.A: A safety helmet for the rider
options.B: A refund of the daily late fee
options.D: A discount at Anderson Hall desk

questions.explanation: The notice states that the standard semester pass costs $45.00 and includes a safety helmet. The other options mix real terms from the text into relationships the notice never establishes.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a rider who damages a bicycle through normal use?

options.A: They forfeit the entire $20.00 deposit amount
options.B: They must purchase a new semester pass first
options.C: They avoid repair charges by reporting it quickly
options.D: They are banned from the program until spring

questions.explanation: The notice says riders who report a mechanical problem promptly will not be charged for repairs, so reporting damage quickly avoids charges. The other options describe penalties the notice does not state.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To warn riders about a new $10.00 late fee
options.B: To inform students about the spring rental program
options.C: To explain why Friday maintenance is necessary
options.D: To request volunteers for the transportation office

questions.explanation: The notice announces the spring reopening and lays out the rules, fees, and contact information, so its purpose is to inform students about the program. The late fee and Friday maintenance are single details, and no volunteers are requested.
